title: becium capitatum
intro: Becium capitatum, also known as Lippia or Wild Basil, is a perennial herb native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and wounds. The health benefits of Becium capitatum include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Becium capitatum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and other health problems.
